title: Authors and Citation
text: Jesse Alderliesten. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Alderliesten J (2026). progutils: Programming Utilities. R package version 0.3.0, https://github.com/JesseAlderliesten/progutils.
code: @Manual{,   title = {progutils: Programming Utilities},   author = {Jesse Alderliesten},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.3.0},   url = {https://github.com/JesseAlderliesten/progutils}, }

title: Programming Utilities
text: progutils contains utility functions, example reorder values, check equality, construct messages, handle files directories.

title: Programming Utilities
text: can visit progutils website explore package. use progutils, install GitHub using following R code (need run R administrator): information installing configuring R RStudio, see package checkrpkgs.
code: if(!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly = TRUE)) {   install.packages(pkgs = "remotes", quiet = FALSE) } remotes::install_github(repo = "JesseAlderliesten/progutils",                         dependencies = NA, upgrade = FALSE, force = FALSE,                         quiet = FALSE, build_vignettes = TRUE, lib = NULL,                         verbose = getOption("verbose"))

title: Programming Utilities
text: Every , reading data R results factor converted numeric vector. code illustrates using .numeric(x) base R work achieve using .numeric_safe() progutils work. Similarly, using relevel levels() base R reorder factor levels work, whereas using reorder_levels() progutils : use named numeric vector subtitle plot, converted character string wrapped appropriate length, prevent text running plot. progutils provides way achieve combining vect_to_char() wrap_text():
code: library(progutils) x <- factor(11:13) x #> [1] 11 12 13 #> Levels: 11 12 13 as.numeric(x) # Returns the indices instead of the values! #> [1] 1 2 3 as.numeric_safe(x) # Returns the values. #> [1] 11 12 13 library(progutils) f_orig <- factor(letters[c(12:13, 13:11)], levels = letters[13:11]) f_orig #> [1] l m m l k #> Levels: m l k  f_test <- f_orig levels(f_test) <- letters[11:13] f_test # Also changes the values! #> [1] l k k l m #> Levels: k l m reorder_levels(x = f_orig, new_order = letters[11:13]) # Only changes the levels #> [1] l m m l k #> Levels: k l m library(progutils) msg_part_one <- "Some\nmessage you want to display" num_vect <- c(j = 10, k = 11, l = 12)  cat(wrap_text(paste(msg_part_one, vect_to_char(num_vect)), width = 20)) #> Some message you #> want to display j: #> 10, k: 11, l: 12  # Retaining newlines that were present in the original message: cat(wrap_text(paste(msg_part_one, vect_to_char(num_vect)), width = 20,               ignore_newlines = FALSE)) #> Some #> message you want to #> display j: 10, k: #> 11, l: 12

title: Programming Utilities
text: Many packages miscellaneous functions exist GitHub CRAN: frequently ‘misc’ ‘util’ title description. addition, many packages utils folder R.

what: Are numeric values nearly equal — are_equal
title: Are numeric values nearly equal — are_equal
text: Test element-wise near-equality numeric vectors allowing small numeric errors make are_equal() safer ==.

title: Are numeric values nearly equal — are_equal
code: are_equal(x, y, tol = sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))

title: Are numeric values nearly equal — are_equal
text: x, y Numeric vectors compare equality. tol small positive number. Numbers differ less tol considered equal.

title: Are numeric values nearly equal — are_equal
text: vector logical values (TRUE, FALSE NA) indicating elements x y equal . NA returned comparisons involving numeric NAs (.e., NA_integer_ NA_real_), NaNs, infinite values sign.

title: Are numeric values nearly equal — are_equal
text: Code abs(x - y) < tol taken dplyr::near().

title: Are numeric values nearly equal — are_equal
code: x <- sqrt(2)^2 x == 2 # FALSE #> [1] FALSE x - 2 # about 4.44e-16 #> [1] 4.440892e-16 are_equal(x = x, y = 2) # TRUE #> [1] TRUE  are_equal(x = c(2, 3, 3,        NA, Inf),           y = c(2, 3, 3 + 1e-8, NA, Inf)) #> [1] TRUE TRUE TRUE   NA   NA are_equal(x = 3, y = c(2, 3, 3 + 1e-8, NA, Inf)) #> [1] FALSE  TRUE  TRUE    NA FALSE

title: Convert x to numeric — as.numeric_safe
text: Convert x vector numeric mode way works x factor, character vector, already numeric vector.

title: Convert x to numeric — as.numeric_safe
code: as.numeric_safe(x, keep_integer = TRUE)

title: Convert x to numeric — as.numeric_safe
text: x factor, character numeric vector converted numeric vector. keep_integer TRUE FALSE: return input type integer without converting double.

title: Convert x to numeric — as.numeric_safe
text: numeric vector, possibly numeric(0) containing NA_real_.

title: Convert x to numeric — as.numeric_safe
text: type integers kept integer keep_integer TRUE changed double keep_integer FALSE. NULL zero-length vectors converted numeric(0). Logical vectors length larger zero converted vector NA_real_, warning. .numeric_safe() uses suggestion factor() R FAQ 7.10 works x factor, character vector already numeric vector (even though last example .numeric() comments approach less efficient long vectors). contrast, alternative conversions .numeric(levels(x))[x] (suggested Warning factor()) .numeric(levels(x))[.integer(x)] (suggested R FAQ 7.10 linked ) work x factor, see Examples. Furthermore, .numeric() used convert factor numeric, returns underlying numeric integer representation (.e., indices factor levels), see Warnings .numeric() factor().

title: Convert x to numeric — as.numeric_safe
code: x_int <- 5:7 x_num <- as.numeric(x_int) x_char <- as.character(x_int) x_fact <- as.factor(x_int)  str(as.numeric_safe(x_int, keep_integer = TRUE)) #>  int [1:3] 5 6 7 str(as.numeric_safe(x_int, keep_integer = FALSE)) #>  num [1:3] 5 6 7  str_as_num_safe <- function(x) {str(as.numeric_safe(x))} str_as_num_fact <- function(x) {str(as.numeric(levels(x))[x])} str_as_num_7.10 <- function(x) {str(as.numeric(levels(x))[as.integer(x)])} str_as_num_base <- function(x) {str(as.numeric(x))}  # as.numeric_safe() works irrespective the type of x str_as_num_safe(x_int)  # correct #>  int [1:3] 5 6 7 str_as_num_safe(x_num)  # correct #>  num [1:3] 5 6 7 str_as_num_safe(x_char) # correct #>  num [1:3] 5 6 7 str_as_num_safe(x_fact) # correct #>  num [1:3] 5 6 7  # The 'more efficient' suggestions in `help(factor)` and # R FAQ 7.10 *only* work for factors. str_as_num_fact(x_int)  # clearly wrong #>  num [1:3] NA NA NA str_as_num_7.10(x_int)  # clearly wrong #>  num [1:3] NA NA NA str_as_num_fact(x_num)  # clearly wrong #>  num [1:3] NA NA NA str_as_num_7.10(x_num)  # clearly wrong #>  num [1:3] NA NA NA str_as_num_fact(x_char) # clearly wrong #>  num [1:3] NA NA NA str_as_num_7.10(x_char) # clearly wrong #>  num [1:3] NA NA NA str_as_num_fact(x_fact) # correct #>  num [1:3] 5 6 7 str_as_num_7.10(x_fact) # correct #>  num [1:3] 5 6 7  # as.numeric() gives the *indices* of the factor levels # for factors str_as_num_base(x_int)  # correct #>  num [1:3] 5 6 7 str_as_num_base(x_num)  # correct #>  num [1:3] 5 6 7 str_as_num_base(x_char) # correct #>  num [1:3] 5 6 7 str_as_num_base(x_fact) # wrong #>  num [1:3] 1 2 3

what: Check for values that differ only in their case — check_case
title: Check for values that differ only in their case — check_case
text: Check values differ case

title: Check for values that differ only in their case — check_case
code: check_case(x, signal = c("error", "warning", "message", "quiet"))

title: Check for values that differ only in their case — check_case
text: x Character vector check. signal Character string indicating type signal used: "error" throw error, "warning" issue warning, "message" show message, "quiet" quiet.

title: Check for values that differ only in their case — check_case
text: Character vector unique, sorted values x differ case, character(0) values present. return invisible.

title: Check for values that differ only in their case — check_case
text: Values x differ case generate error, warning, message signal error, warning, message, respectively. Values silently returned signal quiet.

title: Check for values that differ only in their case — check_case
text: sorting order result depends used locale (see also section Details Comparison), also affects uppercase characters sorted lowercase characters.

title: Check for values that differ only in their case — check_case
code: x <- c("Ee", "Ee", "LL", "Ll") try(check_case(x = x, signal = "error")) #> Error : 'x' contains values that only differ in their case: 'LL', 'Ll' check_case(x = x, signal = "warning") #> Warning: 'x' contains values that only differ in their case: 'LL', 'Ll'

title: Create a directory — create_dir
text: Create directory yet exist.

title: Create a directory — create_dir
code: create_dir(dir = fs::path(".", "output"), add_date = TRUE)

title: Create a directory — create_dir
text: dir Character string containing valid path directory created yet exist. fs::path() adds file separators dot (".") indicates working directory, default subdirectory current date format YYYY_mm_dd directory output working directory created. add_date TRUE FALSE: create subdirectory dir current date format YYYY_mm_dd?

title: Create a directory — create_dir
text: character string absolute normalized path requested directory, returned invisibly. working directory returned attempt create directory fails, warning. happens dir points existing file instead directory.

title: Create a directory — create_dir
text: absolute normalised path returned returned path still works working directory changes. case-insensitive file systems (e.g., Windows macOS), normalization adjusts case match case-insensitive names directories already present (see Examples). "/" instead "\\" used winslash normalisation, returned path can used Windows' file system.

title: Create a directory — create_dir
text: directory indicated returned path created yet exist.

title: Create a directory — create_dir
code: # Use a temporary subdirectory to not write in the user's directory my_tempdir <- fs::path(tempdir(), "testcreatedir")  # Create directory 'dir_one' inside this temporary directory res_dir_one <- create_dir(dir = fs::path(my_tempdir, "dir_one"),                           add_date = FALSE) #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'dir' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/testcreatedir/dir_one dir.exists(res_dir_one) # TRUE #> [1] TRUE  # An attempt to create a directory that already exists does not change any # directory and the same directory is returned. res_dir_one_v2 <- create_dir(dir = fs::path(my_tempdir, "dir_one"),                              add_date = FALSE) #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'dir' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/testcreatedir/dir_one identical(res_dir_one, res_dir_one_v2) # TRUE #> [1] TRUE  # On case-insensitive file systems such as Windows and macOS, adding # 'dir_ONE' to the directory gives the same result as adding 'dir_one' as # done above for 'res_dir_one' res_dir_one_v3 <- create_dir(dir = fs::path(my_tempdir, "dir_ONE"),                              add_date = FALSE) #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'dir' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/testcreatedir/dir_ONE # TRUE on Windows and macOS, FALSE on Ubuntu identical(res_dir_one, res_dir_one_v3) #> [1] FALSE  # Create directory 'dir_two' with a subdirectory containing the current date res_dir_two <- create_dir(dir = fs::path(my_tempdir, "dir_two"),                           add_date = TRUE) #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'dir' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/testcreatedir/dir_two dir.exists(res_dir_two) # TRUE #> [1] TRUE  # Cleaning up unlink(dirname(res_dir_one), recursive = TRUE) rm(my_tempdir, res_dir_one, res_dir_one_v2, res_dir_two, res_dir_one_v3)

title: Create a file path — create_file_path
text: Create file path, creating indicated directory yet exist.

title: Create a file path — create_file_path
code: create_file_path(   filename,   format_stamp = "%Y_%m_%d_%H_%M_%S",   dir = fs::path_wd("output"),   add_date = TRUE )

title: Create a file path — create_file_path
text: filename character string file name, including file extension like .csv .txt. format_stamp character string indicating format stamp added front file name. stamp added format_stamp empty string (.e., ""). formatted stamp treated part filename, restrictions apply, see Details. dir Character string containing valid path directory created yet exist. fs::path() adds file separators dot (".") indicates working directory, default subdirectory current date format YYYY_mm_dd directory output working directory created. add_date TRUE FALSE: create subdirectory dir current date format YYYY_mm_dd?

title: Create a file path — create_file_path
text: created file path, returned invisibly.

title: Create a file path — create_file_path
text: filename contain file extension (.e., dot followed character end file name) contain slashes backslashes: use dir indicate subdirectories. absolute normalised path returned returned path still works working directory changes. "/" instead "\\" used argument winslash returned path can used Windows' file system. warning issued file indicated returned path already exists. prevent creating files quick succession, use "%OSn" part format_stamp create precise stamps truncating seconds 0 <= n <= 6 decimal places, see strftime() details.

title: Create a file path — create_file_path
text: directory indicated returned file path created yet exist.

title: Create a file path — create_file_path
code: # Use a temporary directory to not write in the user's directory my_tempdir <- normalizePath(path = fs::path(tempdir(), "subdir"),                             winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)  (create_file_path(filename = "abc.txt", format_stamp = "",                   dir = my_tempdir, add_date = TRUE)) #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'dir' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'dir' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'file_path' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir/2026_05_31/abc.txt #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir/2026_05_31/abc.txt" (create_file_path(filename = "abc.txt", format_stamp = "%d_%m_%Y",                   dir = my_tempdir, add_date = TRUE)) #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'dir' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'dir' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'file_path' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir/2026_05_31/31_05_2026_abc.txt #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir/2026_05_31/31_05_2026_abc.txt" (create_file_path(filename = "def.html", format_stamp = "",                   dir = my_tempdir, add_date = FALSE)) #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'dir' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'dir' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'file_path' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir/def.html #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir/def.html" (create_file_path(filename = "def.html", format_stamp = "%d_%m_%Y",                   dir = my_tempdir, add_date = FALSE)) #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'dir' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'dir' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'file_path' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir/31_05_2026_def.html #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir/31_05_2026_def.html" (create_file_path(filename = "abc.txt", format_stamp = "",                   dir = fs::path(my_tempdir, "subdir"), add_date = TRUE)) #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'dir' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir/subdir #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'dir' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir/subdir #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'file_path' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir/subdir/2026_05_31/abc.txt #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir/subdir/2026_05_31/abc.txt" (create_file_path(filename = "abc.txt", format_stamp = "%d_%m_%Y",                   dir = fs::path(my_tempdir, "subdir"), add_date = TRUE)) #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'dir' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir/subdir #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'dir' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir/subdir #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'file_path' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir/subdir/2026_05_31/31_05_2026_abc.txt #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir/subdir/2026_05_31/31_05_2026_abc.txt" (create_file_path(filename = "def.html", format_stamp = "",                   dir = fs::path(my_tempdir, "subdir"), add_date = FALSE)) #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'dir' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir/subdir #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'dir' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir/subdir #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'file_path' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir/subdir/def.html #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir/subdir/def.html" (create_file_path(filename = "def.html", format_stamp = "%d_%m_%Y",                   dir = fs::path(my_tempdir, "subdir"), add_date = FALSE)) #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'dir' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir/subdir #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'dir' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir/subdir #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'file_path' will be ignored: #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir/subdir/31_05_2026_def.html #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/subdir/subdir/31_05_2026_def.html"  # Cleaning up unlink(x = my_tempdir, recursive = TRUE) rm(my_tempdir)

title: Create a temporary directory — create_tempdir
code: create_tempdir(subdir = "subdir")

text: subdir character string name -yet existing temporary subdirectory created inside tempdir(). subdir valid path.

title: Create a temporary directory — create_tempdir
text: normalized path created temporary directory, returned invisibly.

title: Create a temporary directory — create_tempdir
text: subdir created inside tempdir() error thrown already exists creating directory fails. ensures removing created directory remove files still needed processes (see Usage practice ). possible create subdirectories inside -yet existing directory (e.g., create <tempdir>/output/outputsub <tempdir>/output yet exist.

title: Create a temporary directory — create_tempdir
text: temporary directory indicated returned path created yet exist.

title: Create a temporary directory — create_tempdir
text: Examples tests write working directory temporary directory cleaned afterwards. Although tempdir() points temporary directory, directory removed RStudio also uses . Instead, store paths temporary files written tempdir() unlink paths cleaning , create temporary subdirectory tempdir() can completely removed cleaning . use_tempdir() follows latter approach.

title: Create a temporary directory — create_tempdir
code: tempdir() #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4" # Create a directory inside the directory returned by 'tempdir()' (tempdir_std <- create_tempdir(subdir = "examplesubtempdir")) #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/examplesubtempdir"  # Error if the directory already exists try(create_tempdir(subdir = "examplesubtempdir")) #> Error in create_tempdir(subdir = "examplesubtempdir") :  #>   Temporary directory already exists: change 'subdir' ('examplesubtempdir'): #> /tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/examplesubtempdir  # It is possible to create recursive directories (tempdir_recursive <- create_tempdir(subdir = fs::path("abc", "def"))) #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/abc/def"  # Clean up unlink(c(tempdir_std, dirname(tempdir_recursive)), recursive = TRUE) rm(tempdir_recursive, tempdir_std)

what: Check that only a single matching file is present — get_file_path
title: Check that only a single matching file is present — get_file_path
text: Check one file directory name matching pattern, example attempting read file.

title: Check that only a single matching file is present — get_file_path
code: get_file_path(dir = ".", pattern, ignore_case = TRUE, quietly = FALSE)

title: Check that only a single matching file is present — get_file_path
text: dir Character string path directory. pattern Character string containing regular expression used select names files present dir. ignore_case TRUE FALSE: use case-insensitive pattern matching? quietly TRUE FALSE: suppress message found file name?

title: Check that only a single matching file is present — get_file_path
text: character string normalized path file name matching pattern exactly one file directory dir. Otherwise error thrown. Use basename() result obtain filename .

title: Check that only a single matching file is present — get_file_path
text: default dir (".") indicates working directory. ignore_case FALSE case-sensitive match found, error message indicates case-insensitive match present. contrast default list.files(), get_file_path() also finds 'hidden' files, .e., files names start dot. Paths normalized ensure still work working directory changes. "/" instead "\\" used argument winslash returned path can used Windows' file system.

title: Check that only a single matching file is present — get_file_path
code: # Create files in a temporary directory so we know what is present. my_tempfiles <- tempfile(pattern = c("some_filename", "another_filename"),                          fileext = ".txt") # Create the files file.create(my_tempfiles) #> [1] TRUE TRUE  get_file_path(dir = tempdir(), pattern = "some_file") #> Using file '/tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/some_filename19672c0be32d.txt' #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/some_filename19672c0be32d.txt"  # The same file is found if case-insensitive matching is used: get_file_path(dir = tempdir(), pattern = "SOME_FILE", ignore_case = TRUE) #> Using file '/tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/some_filename19672c0be32d.txt' #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4/some_filename19672c0be32d.txt"  # Error reporting the presence of a case-insensitive match. try(get_file_path(dir = tempdir(), pattern = "SOME_FILE", ignore_case = FALSE)) #> Error in get_file_path(dir = tempdir(), pattern = "SOME_FILE", ignore_case = FALSE) :  #>   No case-sensitive matches to pattern 'SOME_FILE' are present in directory #> '/tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4'. #> However, a case-insensitive match to 'pattern' is present: 'some_filename19672c0be32d.txt'.  # Error reporting no match found. try(get_file_path(dir = tempdir(), pattern = "missing_filename_abcde",                  ignore_case = TRUE)) #> Error in get_file_path(dir = tempdir(), pattern = "missing_filename_abcde",  :  #>   No matches to pattern 'missing_filename_abcde' are present in directory #> '/tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4'. try(get_file_path(dir = tempdir(), pattern = "missing_filename_abcde",                  ignore_case = FALSE)) #> Error in get_file_path(dir = tempdir(), pattern = "missing_filename_abcde",  :  #>   No case-sensitive matches to pattern 'missing_filename_abcde' are present in directory #> '/tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4'. #> No case-insensitive match is present either.  # Error if multiple matches are present. try(get_file_path(dir = tempdir(), pattern = "_filename")) #> Error in get_file_path(dir = tempdir(), pattern = "_filename") :  #>   Multiple matches to pattern '_filename' are present in directory #> '/tmp/RtmpSy8iZ4': 'another_filename19676242994b.txt', 'some_filename19672c0be32d.txt'!  # Clean up unlink(x = my_tempfiles) rm(my_tempfiles)

what: Show first and last part of an object — head_tail
title: Show first and last part of an object — head_tail
text: Show first last part object

title: Show first and last part of an object — head_tail
code: head_tail(x, n = 3L)

title: Show first and last part of an object — head_tail
text: x object first last part returned. n natural number (default 3L) indicating number elements rows return head tail.

title: Show first and last part of an object — head_tail
text: n first last elements rows x.

title: Show first and last part of an object — head_tail
code: x <- letters[1:10] names(x) <- LETTERS[1:10] x #>   A   B   C   D   E   F   G   H   I   J  #> "a" "b" "c" "d" "e" "f" "g" "h" "i" "j"  head_tail(x) #>   A   B   C   H   I   J  #> "a" "b" "c" "h" "i" "j"  head_tail(x, n = 4) #>   A   B   C   D   G   H   I   J  #> "a" "b" "c" "d" "g" "h" "i" "j"  head_tail(x, n = 40) #>   A   B   C   D   E   F   G   H   I   J  #> "a" "b" "c" "d" "e" "f" "g" "h" "i" "j"  try(head_tail(x, n = 0)) #> Error in checkinput::make_natural(n, strict = TRUE) :  #>   checkinput::is_natural(n) is not TRUE try(head_tail(x, n = 4.1)) #> Error in checkinput::make_natural(n, strict = TRUE) :  #>   checkinput::is_natural(n) is not TRUE  mat <- head_tail(matrix(data = 1:40, ncol = 4,                        dimnames = list(LETTERS[1:10], letters[1:4]))) head_tail(mat) #>    a  b  c  d #> A  1 11 21 31 #> B  2 12 22 32 #> C  3 13 23 33 #> H  8 18 28 38 #> I  9 19 29 39 #> J 10 20 30 40

text: TRUE: error occurs path valid path path contains file extension filename valid.

title: Check that x is a valid path — is_path
text: is_path() puts restrictions paths can used check valid paths creating directory file: path contain characters ", *, ?, |, <, >, control characters (ASCII octal codes 000 037 177, see help("regex")). path components (.e., parts separated file separators / \\) Windows-reserved terms CON, PRN, AUX, NUL, COM<non-zero digit>, LPT<non-zero digit>, case-insensitive variants names, names followed extension. path components end space dot ("." ".." allowed first component indicate working directory parent directory, respectively). path point tempdir(): temporary subdirectory used instead (see create_tempdir()). Furthermore, path contains file extension compression extension, part last slash considered filename, adhere restrictions path contain : start space, might contain Windows-reserved terms. restrictions path filename consider characters lead error Windows allowed; characters lead mismatch created directory returned path silently removed Windows; words reserved names Windows. path point existing directory: path even contain file separator (e.g., / \\), is_path() can used check input fs::path() contains allowed characters. Repeated file separators (e.g., // \\\\) treated single separators, warning.

title: Check that x is a valid path — is_path
text: MacOS, output tempdir() preceded duplicated forward slashes R cmd checks (e.g., /var/[...]/T//RtmpxC2Fyl/working_dir/RtmpdnqgUR), leading spurious warning is_path(). file separator backslash (\) Windows forward slash (/) operating systems (.Platform$file.sep gives file separator used current platform). Furthermore, backslash used escape character R, backslashes need escaped R code. Thus, warn string contains file separator, one write warning message warning("Repeated '/' '\\'") printed Repeated '/' '\'. Checks presence slashes backslashes use grepl(pattern = "/", x = string) grepl(pattern = "\\\\", x = string). makes cumbersome get correct type number slashes compare path recorded warning message, robust check fixed parts message (e.g., "Repeated"), possibly followed check like tinytest::expect_true(dir.exists(string)). Trailing slashes backslashes allowed, even though might removed operations.

title: Check that x is a valid path — is_path
text: Naming files, paths, namespaces Microsoft Comparison file systems Wikipedia

title: Check that x is a valid path — is_path
code: is_path(getwd()) #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'getwd()' will be ignored: #> /home/runner/work/progutils/progutils/docs/reference #> [1] TRUE is_path(fs::path_wd("abcd")) #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'fs::path_wd("abcd")' will be ignored: #> /home/runner/work/progutils/progutils/docs/reference/abcd #> [1] TRUE try(is_path(fs::path_wd("ab|cd"))) #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'fs::path_wd("ab|cd")' will be ignored: #> /home/runner/work/progutils/progutils/docs/reference/ab|cd #> Error in is_path(fs::path_wd("ab|cd")) :  #>   'fs::path_wd("ab|cd")' should not contain '"', '*', '?', '|', '<' or '>': #> /home/runner/work/progutils/progutils/docs/reference/ab|cd  is_path(fs::path_wd("abcd.txt")) #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'fs::path_wd("abcd.txt")' will be ignored: #> /home/runner/work/progutils/progutils/docs/reference/abcd.txt #> [1] TRUE is_path(fs::path_wd("abcd.txt.gz")) #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'fs::path_wd("abcd.txt.gz")' will be ignored: #> /home/runner/work/progutils/progutils/docs/reference/abcd.txt.gz #> [1] TRUE is_path(fs::path_wd("abcd.gz")) #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'fs::path_wd("abcd.gz")' will be ignored: #> /home/runner/work/progutils/progutils/docs/reference/abcd.gz #> [1] TRUE  try(is_path(fs::path_wd("ab:cd.txt"))) #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'fs::path_wd("ab:cd.txt")' will be ignored: #> /home/runner/work/progutils/progutils/docs/reference/ab:cd.txt #> Error in is_path(fs::path_wd("ab:cd.txt")) :  #>   'filename' should not contain ':': #> ab:cd.txt try(is_path(fs::path_wd("ab|cd.txt"))) #> Warning: Repeated '/' or '\\' in 'fs::path_wd("ab|cd.txt")' will be ignored: #> /home/runner/work/progutils/progutils/docs/reference/ab|cd.txt #> Error in is_path(fs::path_wd("ab|cd.txt")) :  #>   'fs::path_wd("ab|cd.txt")' should not contain '"', '*', '?', '|', '<' or '>': #> /home/runner/work/progutils/progutils/docs/reference/ab|cd.txt

title: Are values absent — not_in
text: Check values one vector absent another vector

title: Are values absent — not_in
code: not_in(x, table, value = TRUE)

title: Are values absent — not_in
text: x Vector factor values test absence table. x length larger zero type double. table Vector factor test absence x. table length larger zero type double. value TRUE FALSE: vector values returned instead boolean vector?

title: Are values absent — not_in
text: value TRUE: values x absent table, zero-length object type x, e.g., character(0) logical(0) none values x absent table (.e., present table). value FALSE: boolean vector indicating element x absent table.

title: Are values absent — not_in
text: Duplicates x kept, contrast setdiff(), see Examples. Factor-input x converted character, prevent returning factor values x levels. NAs allowed x table behave values: returned NAs (value TRUE) returned zero-length value (value FALSE) type NAs x NAs absent table. NAs different types x table match . Names considered matching retained output, similar %%.

title: Are values absent — not_in
text: not_in() allow input type double matching input take small numerical errors account using tolerance, example, error message indicates, using are_equal(). not_in() allow zero-length input zero-length input behaves slightly different values: character(0) present x absent table, not_in() return logical(0) value FALSE. value TRUE, behaviour normal: returning character(0). Apart allowing numeric zero-length input, not_in(x, table, value = FALSE) equivalent x %notin% table, %notin% function base R since version 4.6.0.

title: Are values absent — not_in
code: x <- letters[1:4] table <- letters[3:6] not_in(x, table) # c("a", "b") #> [1] "a" "b" not_in(as.factor(x), as.factor(table)) # c("a", "b") #> [1] "a" "b" # c(TRUE, TRUE, FALSE, FALSE), same as !(x %in% table): not_in(x, table, value = FALSE) #> [1]  TRUE  TRUE FALSE FALSE  x_dupl <- c(x, letters[c(2, 4:6, 5)]) table_dupl <- letters[c(3:8, 5:7)] not_in(x_dupl, table_dupl) # c("a", "b", "b") #> [1] "a" "b" "b" setdiff(x_dupl, table_dupl) # c("a", "b") #> [1] "a" "b" not_in(x_dupl, table_dupl, value = FALSE) #> [1]  TRUE  TRUE FALSE FALSE  TRUE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE # c(TRUE, TRUE, FALSE, FALSE, TRUE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE)  # Names are not considered when matching but are retained in the output not_in(c(x = "c", y = "b", z = "a"), c(a = "a", b = "b")) #>   x  #> "c"

title: progutils: Programming Utilities — progutils-package

what: Objects exported from other packages — reexports

text: objects imported packages. Follow links see documentation. checkinput paste_quoted()

title: Reorder columns — reorder_cols
text: Reorder columns

title: Reorder columns — reorder_cols
code: reorder_cols(x, new_order)

title: Reorder columns — reorder_cols
text: x Object least one column. Columns unique, syntactically valid names, see checkinput::all_names(). new_order Character vector length larger zero containing unique names new order columns.

title: Reorder columns — reorder_cols
text: x reordered columns.

title: Reorder columns — reorder_cols
text: Column names x missing new_order appended new_order, warning. Values new_order missing column names x dropped, warning.

title: Reorder columns — reorder_cols
code: test_df <- data.frame(a = 1:2, b = 11:12, c = 21:22) reorder_cols(x = test_df, new_order = c("b", "a", "c")) #>    b a  c #> 1 11 1 21 #> 2 12 2 22  # reorder_cols() appends column names that are missing from 'new_order' and # drops values from 'new_order' that are missing from column names, both with # a warning. reorder_cols(x = test_df, new_order = c("b", "a", "d")) #> Warning: Appended columns that are present in 'x' but missing from 'new_order': #> 'c' #> Warning: Dropped values of 'new_order' that are not present in column names of 'x': #> 'd' #>    b a  c #> 1 11 1 21 #> 2 12 2 22

title: Reorder factor levels — reorder_levels
text: Reorder factor levels

title: Reorder factor levels — reorder_levels
code: reorder_levels(x, new_order, warn_drop_order = TRUE)

title: Reorder factor levels — reorder_levels
text: x Factor levels reordered new_order, character vector converted factor levels ordered new_order. length larger zero. new_order Unique character vector length larger zero indicating new order factor levels. warn_drop_order TRUE FALSE: warn values new_order dropped present x?

title: Reorder factor levels — reorder_levels
text: x converting factor levels reordered new_order, dropping values new_order present x.

title: Reorder factor levels — reorder_levels
text: Character input x silently converted factor reordering factor levels. Values factor x present levels added levels, warning. Levels factor x present values dropped, warning. Levels x missing new_order appended new_order, warning. Values new_order missing levels x dropped, warning warn_drop_order TRUE.

title: Reorder factor levels — reorder_levels
text: Reordering levels factor f replacing levels code like levels(f) <- levels(f)[<order>] work, see last Example.

title: Reorder factor levels — reorder_levels
text: also use approach like levels(x) <- list("Yes" = c("Y", "Yes"), "" = c("N", "")), see levels().

title: Reorder factor levels — reorder_levels
code: orig <- factor(letters[c(12:13, 13:11)], levels = letters[13:11]) orig #> [1] l m m l k #> Levels: m l k reorder_levels(x = orig, new_order = letters[11:13]) #> [1] l m m l k #> Levels: k l m  # Changing the levels directly does *not* work because it changes the values levels(orig) <- letters[11:13] orig #> [1] l k k l m #> Levels: k l m

what: Replace character values or factor levels — replace_vals
title: Replace character values or factor levels — replace_vals
text: Replace character values factor levels

title: Replace character values or factor levels — replace_vals
code: replace_vals(   x,   old,   new,   ignore_case = FALSE,   allow_multiple = TRUE,   warn_absent = TRUE,   signal_case_old = c("warning", "error", "message", "quiet"),   signal_case_new = c("warning", "error", "message", "quiet"),   signal_old_ignore_case = TRUE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Replace character values or factor levels — replace_vals
text: x Character vector values replaced, factor levels replaced. old Character vector values replaced. unique contain value new, otherwise error thrown. new Character string length one new value. ignore_case TRUE FALSE: ignore case looking old? See Details. allow_multiple TRUE FALSE: allow multiple values old match x? See Details. warn_absent TRUE FALSE: warn old new found x? silently assumed replacement necessary anymore new found. signal_case_old, signal_case_new "error", "warning", "message", "quiet": character string indicating type signal values x case-insensitive match case-sensitive match old new, respectively. signal_case_old, also influenced argument signal_old_ignore_case. signal_old_ignore_case TRUE FALSE: use signal_case_old ignore_case TRUE? FALSE, signal emitted ignore_case TRUE. quiet TRUE FALSE: suppress printing message values replaced?

title: Replace character values or factor levels — replace_vals
text: x requested replacements. Factor levels reordered replacement.

title: Replace character values or factor levels — replace_vals
text: Values x differ new case adjusted, lead signal indicated argument signal_case_new. error thrown allow_multiple FALSE multiple values old match x, unless values old differ case ignore_case TRUE. example exception replace_vals(x = c("", ""), old = c("", ""), new = "b", ignore_case = TRUE, allow_multiple = FALSE). quiet FALSE, message indicates values replaced. order factor levels determines order used message.

title: Replace character values or factor levels — replace_vals
code: x <- c("k", "l", "m")  # All values in 'x' that match any value of 'old' are replaced by 'new'. replace_vals(x = rep(x, 2), old = x[3:2], new = "b", allow_multiple = TRUE) #> Replaced values 'l', 'm' with 'b' #> [1] "k" "b" "b" "k" "b" "b"  # Factor input to 'x' is handled by replacing the values and levels. The # levels are not reordered replace_vals(x = as.factor(x), old = x[3:2], new = "b", allow_multiple = TRUE) #> Replaced values 'l', 'm' with 'b' #> [1] k b b #> Levels: k b  # Case-insensitive matching is used if 'ignore_case' is TRUE, with a warning # if 'signal_case_old' is '"warning"' replace_vals(x = "A", old = "a", new = "b", ignore_case = TRUE,              signal_case_old = "warning") #> Warning: Values in 'x' are a case-insensitive match but not a case-sensitive match to #> 'old' ('a'): 'A' #> Replaced values 'A' with 'b' #> [1] "b"  # Case-sensitive matching is used if 'ignore_case' is FALSE. A warning is # issued if no match is found and 'warn_absent' is 'TRUE' replace_vals(x = "A", old = "a", new = "b", ignore_case = FALSE,              warn_absent = TRUE) #> Warning: Values in 'x' are a case-insensitive match but not a case-sensitive match to #> 'old' ('a'): 'A' #> Warning: None of the values of argument 'old' ('a') were found in 'x' ('A')! #> [1] "A"  # If 'allow_multiple' is FALSE, an error is thrown if multiple values of # 'old' match 'x'. try(replace_vals(x = x, old = letters[13:12], new = "b",                  allow_multiple = FALSE)) #> Error in replace_vals(x = x, old = letters[13:12], new = "b", allow_multiple = FALSE) :  #>   Multiple values of 'old' ('m', 'l') matched 'x' ('k', 'l', 'm'): 'l', 'm'

what: Signal a text to the user — signal_text
title: Signal a text to the user — signal_text
text: Signal text user error, warning, message.

title: Signal a text to the user — signal_text
code: signal_text(   text,   signal = c("error", "warning", "message", "quiet"),   origin = character(0) )

title: Signal a text to the user — signal_text
text: text Vector text signalled, coerced character vect_to_char(). signal Character string indicating type signal used: "error" throw error, "warning" issue warning, "message" show message, "quiet" quiet. origin Character string pasted behind text giving origin message. Ignored character(0).

title: Signal a text to the user — signal_text
text: text, returned invisibly.

title: Signal a text to the user — signal_text
text: text coerced character vector using vect_to_char(), treats zero-length input input length larger one better message() etc. use paste0(), see Examples. Call vect_to_char() beforehand text control rounding wrapping, see last example. text signalled error, warning, message, quietly, depending argument signal.

title: Signal a text to the user — signal_text
code: test_text <- c("Some text", "Some other text") try(signal_text(text = test_text, signal = "error")) #> Error : Some text, Some other text signal_text(text = test_text, signal = "message") #> Some text, Some other text signal_text(text = test_text, signal = "quiet")  # signal_text() handles input to 'text' with length larger than one more # sensible than message(): test_numbers <- 11:13 signal_text(text = test_numbers, signal = "message") #> 11, 12, 13 message(test_numbers) #> 111213  # Call vect_to_char() beforehand on 'text' to control rounding of numbers and # wrapping of text: message(test_numbers / 7) #> 1.571428571428571.714285714285711.85714285714286 signal_text(text = test_numbers / 7, signal = "message") #> 1.57, 1.71, 1.86 signal_text(text = vect_to_char(x = test_numbers / 7, signif = 4, width = 15),             signal = "message") #> 1.571, 1.714, #> 1.857

what: Create a character vector from a string with quotation marks — unpaste_unquote
title: Create a character vector from a string with quotation marks — unpaste_unquote
text: Create character vector string quotation marks

title: Create a character vector from a string with quotation marks — unpaste_unquote
code: unpaste_unquote(x, collapse = c(", ", "; "), quotemarks = c("'", "\""))

title: Create a character vector from a string with quotation marks — unpaste_unquote
text: x character string collapse Character vector elements used collapse values x created, now used split x . Can character(0) leave x character string. quotemarks Character vector quotation marks removed x. Can character(0) remove quotation marks.

title: Create a character vector from a string with quotation marks — unpaste_unquote
text: x without quotation marks split vector.

title: Create a character vector from a string with quotation marks — unpaste_unquote
text: unpaste_unquote() exact reverse paste_quoted(): restore zero-length elements original value, e.g., "'NULL'" NULL, "'character(0)'" character(0).

title: Create a character vector from a string with quotation marks — unpaste_unquote
code: x <- paste_quoted(c("ff", "gG", "HH")) x #> [1] "'ff', 'gG', 'HH'" unpaste_unquote(x = x, collapse = ", ", quotemarks = "'") #> [1] "ff" "gG" "HH" unpaste_unquote(x = x, collapse = ", ", quotemarks = "\"") #> [1] "'ff'" "'gG'" "'HH'" unpaste_unquote(x = x, collapse = character(0), quotemarks = c("'", "\"")) #> [1] "ff, gG, HH" unpaste_unquote(x = x, collapse = character(0), quotemarks = character(0)) #> [1] "'ff', 'gG', 'HH'"

what: Convert a vector to a character string — vect_to_char
title: Convert a vector to a character string — vect_to_char
text: Convert vector character string preserving names rounding numeric values.

title: Convert a vector to a character string — vect_to_char
code: vect_to_char(   x,   signif = 3L,   width = Inf,   sep = ": ",   collapse = ", ",   ignore_newlines = TRUE )

title: Convert a vector to a character string — vect_to_char
text: x vector, factor, NULL, non-dataframe list (unlisted unlist(x, use.names = TRUE), warning). signif Positive number length one, rounded nearest positive integer indicating number significant digits round numeric x . width positive number giving maximum line width (characters) wrapping. Can Inf wrap text. sep Character string length one used separate names values. Ignored x names. collapse Character string length one collapse values single character string, NULL return value element character vector. ignore_newlines TRUE FALSE: newlines x replaced blank character?

title: Convert a vector to a character string — vect_to_char
text: names values x, values numeric x rounded signif significant digits, wrapped width characters. collapse NULL, character vector elements x returned, wrapping per-element basis. collapse NULL, name-value pairs separated collapse, thus returning character string. See Details handling special values.

title: Convert a vector to a character string — vect_to_char
text: vect_to_char() returns NULL "NULL", zero-length objects "<class>(0)" (e.g., "logical(0)"), "" '""', non-logical NAs "NA_<class>_" (e.g., "NA_real_"; factors "NA_character_").

title: Convert a vector to a character string — vect_to_char
text: get cross-tabulation x character string, one can use paste0(vect_to_char(c(table(x)), sep = " (", collapse =  "), "), ")"), see last Example.

title: Convert a vector to a character string — vect_to_char
code:
x <- 1:3 names(x) <- letters[x] vect_to_char(x = x) # "a: 1, b: 2, c: 3" #> [1] "a: 1, b: 2, c: 3" vect_to_char(x = x, collapse = NULL) # c("a: 1", "b: 2", "c: 3") #> [1] "a: 1" "b: 2" "c: 3" vect_to_char(x = unname(x)) # "1, 2, 3" #> [1] "1, 2, 3" y <- x / 7 vect_to_char(x = y, signif = 7) # "a: 0.1428571, b: 0.2857143, c: 0.4285714" #> [1] "a: 0.1428571, b: 0.2857143, c: 0.4285714" vect_to_char(x = y, signif = 2, sep = " = ", collapse = " and ", width = 15) #> [1] "a = 0.14 and b
= 0.29 and c =
0.43" # "a = 0.14 and b
= 0.29 and c =
0.43"  x_char <- c(a = "abc", b = "def", c = "this is text") vect_to_char(x = x_char) # "a: abc, b: def, c: this is text" #> [1] "a: abc, b: def, c: this is text" vect_to_char(x = unname(x_char)) # "abc, def, this is some text" #> [1] "abc, def, this is text"  # Using vect_to_char() to get a frequency table x <- 1:10 names(x) <- letters[x] y <- 5:15 names(y) <- letters[y] x <- c(x, y) paste0(vect_to_char(c(table(x)), sep = " (", collapse =  "), "), ")") #> [1] "1 (1), 2 (1), 3 (1), 4 (1), 5 (2), 6 (2), 7 (2), 8 (2), 9 (2), 10 (2), 11 (1), 12 (1), 13 (1), 14 (1), 15 (1)"

title: Wrap text — wrap_text
text: Wrap text blank characters achieve maximum line width.

title: Wrap text — wrap_text
code: wrap_text(x, width = 80L, ignore_newlines = TRUE)

title: Wrap text — wrap_text
text: x Character vector wrapped. width positive number giving maximum line width (characters) wrapping. Can Inf wrap text. ignore_newlines TRUE FALSE: newlines x replaced blank character?

title: Wrap text — wrap_text
text: string containing x wrapped maximum width characters, newlines inserted blank characters. warning issued width fragment output exceeds width: occurs stretch characters longer width occurs without blank character wrap .

title: Wrap text — wrap_text
text: x length larger one pasted single string, separating parts blank characters. Consecutive white space collapsed single blank character, except double spaces periods, question marks exclamation marks (documented section Details strwrap()). Leading trailing newlines collapsed single blank character ignore_newlines TRUE retained ignore_newlines FALSE.

title: Wrap text — wrap_text
text: call wrap_text(x, width) can replaced paste0(strwrap(x, width + 1L), collapse = "\n") x length one ignore_newlines TRUE. Using wrap_text() x variable length, e.g., text warnings report file path user-provided variable name, makes location newlines unpredictable thus difficult test reliably. circumvent , put constant part front message hardcode newlines using \n.

title: Wrap text — wrap_text
text: output printed string newlines represented \n. Use cat() output print way formatted messages. Argument width wrap_text() indicates maximum width text wrapping, .e., width text wrapped. contrast, argument width strwrap() indicates width text wrapped.

title: Wrap text — wrap_text
code: example_text <- "A piece\nof text that you want to wrap over multiple lines" cat(wrap_text(example_text,               width = 20, ignore_newlines = TRUE)) #> A piece of text that #> you want to wrap #> over multiple lines cat(wrap_text(example_text,               width = 20, ignore_newlines = FALSE)) #> A piece #> of text that you #> want to wrap over #> multiple lines

title: progutils 0.3.0
text: Added dependency fs. create_file_path(): longer replace non-alphanumeric characters filename reconstituting filename brittle. Replace file_path_no_ext() fs::path_ext_remove() file_path_ext() fs::path_ext(). get_filename(): rename get_file_path() return complete file path instead file name. is_filename(): removed, use is_path(). is_path(): drop argument as_file. Instead, check filename valid file extension present. Replace file.path(".", ...) file.path(getwd(), ...) fs::path_wd(...); replace instances file.path() fs::path().

title: progutils 0.2.0
text: Dependency checkinput: increase minimum version 0.7.0 0.8.0 update argument name allow_zero allow_zero_length.

title: progutils 0.1.0
text: Dependency checkinput: increase minimum version 0.6.0 0.7.0 new default = FALSE instead = TRUE make_natural().

title: progutils 0.0.13
text: .numeric_safe() gains argument keep_integer (default TRUE, contrast implicit prior default FALSE), fixing issue #1.

title: progutils 0.0.12
text: create_path: rename create_file_path. Replace non-alphanumeric characters dots underscores underscores instead replacing non-alphanumeric characters underscores dots.

title: progutils 0.0.12
text: .numeric_safe(): moved Note Details. Condensed example section. create_tempdir(): add section Usage practice explains using create_tempdir() preferable using tempdir(). head_tail(): longer erroneously document n can zero. Removed uninformative example. is_path(): add section Programming notes file separators.

title: progutils 0.0.11
text: create_dir(): use is_filename() check filename valid. create_path: use is_path() check dir valid path. create_tempdir(): write subdirectories tempdir() use is_path() check subdir valid. file_path_sans_ext(): rename file_path_no_ext() distinguish tools::file_path_sans_ext() linking documentation; add argument compression. wrap_text(): collapse leading trailing whitespace , ignore_newlines TRUE, leading trailing newlines single blank character instead removing . Retains leading trailing newlines ignore_newlines FALSE.

title: progutils 0.0.11
text: Add functions file_path_ext(), is_filename() is_path().

title: progutils 0.0.9
text: Dependency checkinput: increase minimum version 0.5.0 0.6.0, needed use re-exported paste_quoted(). progutils::paste_quoted(): move checkinput re-export progutils.

title: progutils 0.0.9
text: Add function create_tempdir() create temporary directory can safely removed.

title: progutils 0.0.8
text: Removed unused check_os_is_windows(). Use roxygen2 version 8.0.0. not_in(): x table vector factor (always documented) prevent returning x x table list.

title: progutils 0.0.8
text: Add head_tail() show first last part object.

title: progutils 0.0.7
text: paste_quoted(): return "" '\"\"' instead ''. Indicate class non-logical NAs. allow list-type x. changes also affect warnings messages functions. replace_vals(): new also processed paste_quoted() message indicating replacement. vect_to_char(): return "" \"\" instead . Indicate class non-logical NAs. change also affects warnings messages functions.

title: progutils 0.0.6
text: Dependency checkinput: increase minimum version 0.1.0 0.5.0. increases minimum version R 4.1.0 removes dependency vctrs. Dependency tinytest: declare version >= 1.4.1 argument strict used expect_message() expect_warning(). create_dir(): dir ending \. now , documented, error instead silently denoting working directory. Hardcode newlines instead using wrap_text() makes easier test warnings.

title: progutils 0.0.5
text: replace_vals(): gained argument signal_old_ignore_case. Bugfix , consistent handling , NA factors. Values messages longer sorted alphabetically.

title: progutils 0.0.4
text: vect_to_char() gained argument ignore_newlines pass wrap_text().

title: progutils 0.0.4
text: check_case: check values differ case. replace_vals: replace character values factor levels. signal_text: signal text user error, warning, message. unpaste_unquote: opposite paste_quoted().

title: progutils 0.0.3
text: checkinput: increased minimum version 0.1.0. Add stats tools dependencies Suggests field linked help pages. longer import methods::formalArgs(). create_dir(): show warnings creating directory fails. create_dir(): use winslash = "/" instead winslash = "\\" normalise paths. get_filename(): use winslash = "/" instead winslash = "\\" normalise paths. get_paths(): removed. Use .libPaths() instead.

title: progutils 0.0.3
text: create_path(): create file paths. file_path_sans_ext(): replacing tools::file_path_sans_ext(), also recognise extension file names end dot. reorder_cols(): reorder columns. reorder_levels(): reorder factor levels.

title: progutils 0.0.3
text: create_dir(): move documentation create_path(); document format date-time stamp case strftime().
